core_mechanism: a nested (secondary) modulational instability in which a localized perturbation grows on a background that is itself the saturated product of a primary instability, nucleating a self-sustained, transient localized coherent excitation (a breather/rogue-wave-type structure) that extracts and is sustained by energy exchange among competing background solitons
brief_reason: The abstract describes Kerr saturation turning exponential parametric growth into a soliton train and then a localized perturbation triggering a transient 'instability on instability' breathing event sustained by interaction with competing host solitons, i.e. a secondary instability layered on an already-unstable nonlinear background.
